findings.reasoning: This page (V68) is a TurboTax Online vs. Desktop comparison page focused on DIY tax filing products, pricing, guarantees, and switching guidance. It does mention Credit Karma Money accounts and MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC in a footnote about the refund advance / 5-days-early feature ('Banking services for Credit Karma Money accounts are provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC'), but this is a passing mention of banking service provider for a refund-delivery feature, not an advertisement of a deposit product itself. The page does not market or feature a deposit account product (e.g., Credit Karma Money Spend/Save account) as its own offering with FDIC insurance amount language; it only references FDIC status of the bank incidentally in the context of refund delivery and loan/router account mechanics. There is a mention of 'authorize Intuit Financing Inc. to open a Router Account in your name with an FDIC insured bank' but again this is not advertising a deposit product for the consumer to open and hold funds in as a marketed feature - it's a mechanism for the File Now Pay Later loan payment. No deposit product with FDIC insurance amount claims (e.g., 'up to $250,000') is being advertised on this page. Therefore, R-03's trigger condition (a deposit product being advertised) is not met on this page. Strongest counter-reading: one could argue the Credit Karma Money account mentioned for refund deposit purposes counts as a 'deposit product' being advertised, which would trigger the rule requiring the specific $250,000 FDIC insurance language. However, the page does not promote opening/using Credit Karma Money as a standalone deposit product; it merely notes the bank behind incidental refund-related features. The FDIC mentions are generic institutional insurance references ('Member FDIC'), not claims about deposit insurance coverage amounts, and no deposit account is being marketed as a product with rates, benefits, or sign-up CTAs. Given this, I conclude the rule is not triggered on this page.

Rule R-03 applies only when a deposit product is being advertised and therefore FDIC insurance language would be required. This page promotes TurboTax Online, TurboTax Desktop, related tax prep guarantees, loans (Refund Advance, File Now Pay Later), and Credit Karma Money account usage, but it does not advertise any deposit account as a standalone product. The only FDIC-related mentions are descriptive, such as “Credit Karma is not a bank. Banking services for Credit Karma Money accounts are provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC” and router accounts at an FDIC insured bank, which are ancillary to loan or refund-disbursement mechanics, not an advertised deposit product with pricing/benefits. Therefore the rule’s trigger is not met, and the correct outcome is not_applicable.

findings.evidence_quote: Get your tax refund up to 5 days early in a Credit Karma Money™ Account: When it’s time to file, have your tax refund direct deposited to a Credit Karma Money™ checking account, and you could receive your funds up to 5 days early. If you choose to pay your tax preparation fee with TurboTax using your federal tax refund or if you choose to take the TurboTax Refund Advance loan, you will not be eligible to receive your refund up to 5 days early with Credit Karma. 5-day early program may change or discontinue at any time. Up to 5 days early access to your federal tax refund is compared to standard tax refund electronic deposit and is dependent on and subject to IRS submitting refund information to the bank before release date. IRS may not submit refund information early. Excludes business tax returns. Credit Karma is not a bank. Banking services for Credit Karma Money accounts are provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC. Maximum balance and transfer limits apply per account. For more information, please visit <https://turbotax.intuit.com/credit-karma-money/>.
findings.location: Disclosures section: TurboTax Online/MOBILE offers & pricing bullets
findings.reasoning: Trigger: The page promotes a deposit product by encouraging use of a Credit Karma Money checking account to get a refund up to 5 days early. Requirement: When a deposit product is advertised, FDIC insurance language must state that the deposit product is FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through [Bank]. The only FDIC reference present is “Member FDIC” tied to MVB Bank, which omits the $250,000 coverage amount and the approved phrasing, so the requirement is not met. Axis A: Non-compliant because the mandated FDIC statement is missing. Axis B: Does not match the approved library wording. Strongest counter-reading: One could argue the page’s primary product is tax software, not a deposit product, or that “Member FDIC” suffices. I reject that because the specific bullet actively markets the Credit Karma Money account benefit, triggering the rule; and “Member FDIC” alone is not equivalent to the required “FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through [Bank]” language.
